text.title: Python Ecosystem Practices Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Repo has both setup.py and pyproject.toml; build backends pick different metadata.
antipattern.root-cause: Migration was halfway and the legacy file was kept 'just in case'.
antipattern.fix: Delete setup.py + setup.cfg. Ensure pyproject.toml has [build-system] + [project] tables fully populated.

antipattern.symptom: Tests pass with editable install but fail in installed wheel because tests imported the working-dir copy of the package, not the installed one.
antipattern.root-cause: Flat layout: top-level <package>/ directory next to tests/.
antipattern.fix: Move package to src/<package>/. Update pyproject [tool.setuptools.packages.find] or hatch config. Re-run pytest with the installed wheel.

antipattern.symptom: Pre-commit reformats a file twice on every commit; CI complains about leftover diffs.
antipattern.root-cause: Both black and ruff format hooks installed.
antipattern.fix: Drop the black hook. Configure ruff format. Run ruff format once to normalise the codebase.

antipattern.symptom: mypy reports 200 warnings on CI; nobody fixes them; new type errors slip in.
antipattern.root-cause: Project runs mypy without --strict; warnings are not enforced.
antipattern.fix: Flip strict=true; fix or # type: ignore[reason] every existing violation; baseline file via mypy --baseline if needed.

antipattern.symptom: Production install picks a new transitive dep version that breaks startup.
antipattern.root-cause: No uv.lock / requirements.txt under version control; floating versions install at deploy.
antipattern.fix: Generate uv.lock (or pip-compile requirements.in -> requirements.txt). Commit the lockfile. Install via pip install -r requirements.txt --require-hashes.
